reasoning:
The resolution of this question depends on the Metaculus criteria for "Weakly General AI" being met by the end of 2025. These criteria are highly strict, particularly the requirement for an AI to possess robotic capabilities sufficient to autonomously assemble a 1:8 scale mechanical model (a Ferrari 312 T4) from human-readable instructions. While cognitive benchmarks (like a Turing test equivalent) are becoming arguably achievable with advanced LLMs, autonomous robotic assembly of complex physical models lags significantly behind software. 

Furthermore, as of May 2025, the Metaculus community itself predicts this milestone will not be reached until late 2026. With only a few months left until the end of 2025, achieving and publicly demonstrating such specific and advanced robotic and cognitive integration is highly unlikely. The Manifold market's 22% estimate likely reflects a long-shot bias and general AI optimism rather than a realistic appraisal of the specific Metaculus criteria. I am predicting 11%, significantly lower than the market, given the proximity of the resolution date and lack of evidence for the required robotics breakthroughs.
